search_terms_used: ultramarathon training physiology systematic review meta-analysis PubMed
ultra running nutrition systematic review PubMed
trail ultramarathon performance factors systematic review
ultra endurance training adaptations physiology review Sports Medicine
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across ultramarathon physiology, nutrition, performance determinants, neuromuscular fatigue, injury epidemiology, long-term health, and sex differences. Anchor papers include two systematic reviews (Garbisu-Hualde 2020, Kelly 2023) and the Hoffman 2020 systematic analysis of the field. Good representation from high-impact journals (Sports Medicine, IJSPP, JISSN). Population coverage is predominantly recreational and mixed-level ultramarathon runners; elite-specific studies are underrepresented. Gap in RCTs — the field is dominated by observational and review literature.

papers.title: State of the Science on Ultramarathon Running After a Half Century: A Systematic Analysis and Commentary
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Systematic analysis of 616 PubMed-indexed ultramarathon publications from 1970-2019, mapping the state of the research field.

papers.title: What are the Limiting Factors During an Ultra-Marathon? A Systematic Review of the Scientific Literature
papers.notes: Systematic review of 23 studies examining performance-limiting factors in ultra-marathons and mountain ultra-marathons (42.2-101 km).

papers.title: Limits of Ultra: Towards an Interdisciplinary Understanding of Ultra-Endurance Running Performance
papers.notes: Interdisciplinary review of physiological, biomechanical, thermoregulatory, gastrointestinal, and psychological factors limiting ultra-endurance running performance.

papers.title: Can neuromuscular fatigue explain running strategies and performance in ultra-marathons?: the flush model
papers.notes: Proposes the 'flush model' of central regulation to explain neuromuscular fatigue, pacing strategies, and performance in ultra-marathon running.

papers.title: Physiological Determinants of Ultramarathon Trail-Running Performance
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Examined how classical physiological fitness measures (VO2max, running economy, body composition) predict performance across 50-km, 80-km, and 160-km trail ultramarathons.

papers.title: Physiology and Pathophysiology in Ultra-Marathon Running
papers.notes: Comprehensive narrative review of ultra-marathon participant characteristics, performance predictors, metabolic demands, and pathophysiological changes during racing.

papers.title: International Society of Sports Nutrition Position Stand: nutritional considerations for single-stage ultra-marathon training and racing
papers.journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
papers.notes: ISSN position stand providing evidence-based nutritional guidelines for single-stage ultra-marathon training and racing, covering macronutrients, hydration, and supplements.

papers.title: Nutrition for Ultramarathon Running: Trail, Track, and Road
papers.journal: International Journal of Sport Nutrition and Exercise Metabolism
papers.notes: Review of nutritional demands and strategies for ultramarathon running across trail, track, and road formats, covering carbohydrate needs, GI issues, and hydration.

papers.title: Is There Evidence for the Development of Sex-Specific Guidelines for Ultramarathon Coaches and Athletes? A Systematic Review
papers.notes: Systematic review of 45 studies comparing male and female ultramarathon runners across physiology, performance, nutrition, and injury to assess evidence for sex-specific guidelines.

papers.title: Epidemiology of Injury and Illness Among Trail Runners: A Systematic Review
papers.notes: Systematic review of 16 studies (8,644 participants) examining injury and illness incidence, prevalence, and risk factors among trail runners.

papers.title: Potential Long-Term Health Problems Associated with Ultra-Endurance Running: A Narrative Review
papers.notes: Narrative review examining potential long-term cardiovascular, renal, musculoskeletal, and gastrointestinal health consequences of ultra-endurance running.
